section: Animals Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide
text: # Animals Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide

section: Animals Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > Scope
text:
## Scope
This guide describes the animals domain with conservative confidence labeling.

section: Animals Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > confirmed_from_screenshot
text:
## confirmed_from_screenshot
- Table exists: animals.
- Confirmed fields: id_animal, type_animal, sexe, age, etat_sante, statut, id_elevage.
- Observed sample values (not exhaustive):
  - type_animal: Hen, Sheep
  - sexe: Female, Male
  - etat_sante: Sick, Healthy
  - statut: For Sale, Retained

section: Animals Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > inferred_from_schema
text:
## inferred_from_schema
- Each animal appears to be linked to an elevage via id_elevage.
- Animal records likely serve as the central entity for cross-domain questions:
  - health status overview,
  - sales/retention monitoring,
  - vaccination follow-up through id_animal.
- etat_sante and statut are likely distinct concepts:
  - etat_sante: health condition label,
  - statut: operational/commercial label.

section: Animals Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > assumed_business_rule
text:
## assumed_business_rule
No hard lifecycle policy is confirmed from screenshots.

Possible workflow scenarios (hypothetical only):
- Animal onboarding and assignment to an elevage.
- Periodic updates to health and operational status.
- Vaccination follow-up through linked records.

These scenarios should not be treated as official policy until validated by the project owner.

section: Animals Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > Typical User Questions (Conservative)
text:
## Typical User Questions (Conservative)
- What fields define an animal record?
- Which values have been observed for type_animal and statut?
- How does an animal connect to elevage and vaccination data?
- Can observed values be treated as complete enums? (answer: no)

section: Animals Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > Multilingual Notes
text:
## Multilingual Notes
- Keep French schema terms in system responses.
- Support bilingual phrasing in retrieval (for example animal type/type_animal, health state/etat_sante).

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register
text: # Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > Purpose
text:
## Purpose
This file is the authoritative guardrail for evidence quality across the full rag workspace.

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > Confidence Taxonomy (Canonical)
text:
## Confidence Taxonomy (Canonical)
- confirmed: directly observed in screenshot evidence.
- inferred: cautious interpretation from schema structure or naming.
- assumed: hypothetical business policy requiring owner validation.

All corpus and scripts must use these exact labels.

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > 1) confirmed
text: ## 1) confirmed

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > 1) confirmed > Confirmed structure
text:
### Confirmed structure
- Three tables are visible: animals, vaccinations, elevage.
- Field lists for all three tables are confirmed.

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > 1) confirmed > Confirmed sample values (non-exhaustive)
text:
### Confirmed sample values (non-exhaustive)
- animals.type_animal: Hen, Sheep
- animals.sexe: Female, Male
- animals.etat_sante: Sick, Healthy
- animals.statut: For Sale, Retained
- vaccinations.vaccine_name: Brucellosis
- vaccinations.status: Scheduled
- elevage.type_elevage: Poultry Farm, Sheep Farm, Bovin Farm
- elevage.etat_elevage: Cleaning, Under Cleaning
- elevage.production: Egg, Milk

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > 2) inferred
text:
## 2) inferred
- Key roles and joins are likely:
  - animals.id_elevage -> elevage.id_elevage
  - vaccinations.id_animal -> animals.id_animal
- date_done/date_next likely carry historical/planning semantics.
- capacite/nombre_animaux likely support occupancy context.

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > 3) assumed
text:
## 3) assumed
No operational policy is officially confirmed for:
- overdue definition,
- mandatory status transitions,
- numeric thresholds,
- compliance criteria,
- severity taxonomy for etat_elevage.

Any such rule remains assumed until business validation.

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > 4) unknowns_to_confirm
text:
## 4) unknowns_to_confirm
1. SQL DDL constraints (PK/FK, nullability, defaults, indexes).
2. Official controlled vocabularies (if any).
3. Date interpretation policy for scheduling and delay.
4. Whether production is categorical, numeric, or both.
5. Any legal/regulatory policy attached to vaccination workflows.

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > 5) corpus_alignment_requirements
text:
## 5) corpus_alignment_requirements
1. No file may present assumed values as confirmed truth.
2. Observed sample values must be marked non-exhaustive.
3. Retrieval metadata must use canonical taxonomy values exactly.
4. Unknown policy questions must trigger transparent uncertainty responses.

section: Assumptions And Gaps - Master Alignment Register > 6) release_readiness_checkpoint
text:
## 6) release_readiness_checkpoint
Before enabling strict business recommendations, confirm:
- DDL export reviewed,
- policy vocabulary validated by owner,
- confidence tagging audited across corpus and chunks.

section: Business Rules - Conservative Revision
text: # Business Rules - Conservative Revision

section: Business Rules - Conservative Revision > Purpose
text:
## Purpose
This document is intentionally split into three levels of confidence to avoid presenting assumptions as confirmed truth.

section: Business Rules - Conservative Revision > Confidence Taxonomy
text:
## Confidence Taxonomy
- confirmed: explicit screenshot evidence.
- inferred: low-risk interpretation from schema and naming.
- assumed: hypothetical policy that requires validation.

section: Business Rules - Conservative Revision > 1) confirmed_structural_rules
text:
## 1) confirmed_structural_rules

1. Table and field existence is confirmed for:
- animals
- vaccinations
- elevage

2. Screenshot-observed sample values are confirmed as samples only (non-exhaustive), including:
- animals.type_animal: Hen, Sheep
- animals.sexe: Female, Male
- animals.etat_sante: Sick, Healthy
- animals.statut: For Sale, Retained
- vaccinations.vaccine_name: Brucellosis
- vaccinations.status: Scheduled
- elevage.type_elevage: Poultry Farm, Sheep Farm, Bovin Farm
- elevage.etat_elevage: Cleaning, Under Cleaning
- elevage.production: Egg, Milk

3. No screenshot evidence confirms that these observed values are fixed enums.

section: Business Rules - Conservative Revision > 2) inferred_low_risk_logic
text:
## 2) inferred_low_risk_logic

1. animals.id_elevage likely links animals to elevage.
2. vaccinations.id_animal likely links vaccinations to animals.
3. date_done and date_next likely represent historical and planning dates.
4. capacite and nombre_animaux likely support capacity monitoring use cases.
5. latitude and longitude likely support map-based visualization.

These inferences are technical and low-risk, but still require SQL DDL validation for strict certainty.

section: Business Rules - Conservative Revision > 3) hypothetical_policies_requiring_validation
text:
## 3) hypothetical_policies_requiring_validation

The following are examples of possible policy logic and MUST be confirmed before production enforcement:

1. Vaccination schedule interpretation
- Example hypothesis: date_next is used for upcoming task planning.
- Validation needed: official rule for due date semantics.

2. Vaccination delay interpretation
- Example hypothesis: if date_next is in the past and action is unfinished, the record is considered delayed.
- Validation needed: official business definition and allowed status labels.

3. Livestock operational interpretation
- Example hypothesis: etat_elevage labels can express operational activity state.
- Validation needed: official vocabulary and governance for state transitions.

4. Animal status governance
- Example hypothesis: statut values may drive retention or sale workflows.
- Validation needed: official compliance criteria and decision policy.

5. Threshold-based alerting
- No numeric threshold is confirmed from screenshots.
- Any threshold (time-based or ratio-based) is assumed until validated.

section: Business Rules - Conservative Revision > Production Safety Notes
text:
## Production Safety Notes
- Do not encode assumed rules as hard logic in retrieval or prompts without owner validation.
- Do not hardcode status vocabularies beyond confirmed samples.
- Keep all unknown policy responses explicit and traceable.

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style
text: # Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Style Contract
text:
## Style Contract
- Ground answers in confirmed or inferred evidence.
- Use confidence labels explicitly when policy is unknown.
- Keep French schema terms visible in answers.

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Beginner Examples
text: ## Beginner Examples

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Beginner Examples > Example B1
text:
### Example B1
User: What is the difference between etat_sante and statut?

Assistant:
- Direct answer: etat_sante appears to represent health condition, while statut appears to represent operational/commercial label.
- Confidence: inferred.
- Note: observed values support this distinction (Sick/Healthy vs For Sale/Retained), but complete vocabularies are not yet confirmed.

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Beginner Examples > Example B2
text:
### Example B2
User: Is Scheduled the only vaccination status?

Assistant:
- Direct answer: only Scheduled is confirmed as an observed sample value.
- Confidence: confirmed for observation, unknown for exhaustiveness.
- Note: additional status values may exist.

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Beginner Examples > Example B3
text:
### Example B3
User: Quelles valeurs sont observees pour etat_elevage ?

Assistant:
- Direct answer: observed sample values include Cleaning and Under Cleaning.
- Confidence: confirmed.
- Note: these are observed samples and not an exhaustive list.

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Intermediate Examples
text: ## Intermediate Examples

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Intermediate Examples > Example I1
text:
### Example I1
User: Can you classify all elevage states as warning or critical?

Assistant:
- Direct answer: no confirmed evidence supports a warning/critical taxonomy in current screenshots.
- Confidence: confirmed absence of evidence.
- Next step: validate official etat_elevage vocabulary with project owner.

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Intermediate Examples > Example I2
text:
### Example I2
User: How should we use date_next?

Assistant:
- Direct answer: date_next is likely intended for future vaccination planning.
- Confidence: inferred.
- Note: exact business policy for reminders or delays is not confirmed yet.

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Advanced Examples
text: ## Advanced Examples

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Advanced Examples > Example A1
text:
### Example A1
User: Give me a strict overdue-vaccination policy.

Assistant:
- Direct answer: a strict policy is not confirmed by current evidence.
- Confidence: assumed.
- Response outcome: unknown_policy.
- Safe guidance: you can define a candidate policy internally, but it must be validated before being treated as canonical.

section: Chat Examples - Conservative Answering Style > Advanced Examples > Example A2
text:
### Example A2
User: Build a cross-entity monitoring view with current evidence only.

Assistant:
- Direct answer: you can safely combine inferred links:
  - elevage to animals via id_elevage,
  - animals to vaccinations via id_animal.
- Confidence: inferred.
- Limitation: PK/FK constraints and official operational thresholds remain unconfirmed.

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned
text: # Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned > Scope
text:
## Scope
This dictionary is intentionally conservative. It separates:
- confirmed examples seen in screenshots,
- inferred meaning from schema naming,
- optional candidate examples that are explicitly unconfirmed.

Important:
- Observed values are samples only, not fixed enums.
- The system must stay open to additional values.

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned > Confidence Taxonomy
text:
## Confidence Taxonomy
- confirmed: explicit screenshot evidence.
- inferred: likely interpretation from schema design.
- assumed: hypothetical usage pattern pending business validation.

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned > Table: animals
text:
## Table: animals

| Field | Business Meaning | confirmed_examples_from_screenshot | candidate_values_unconfirmed | Confidence Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| id_animal | Technical identifier for one animal record | none shown | n/a | inferred as unique key role, not yet DDL-confirmed |
| type_animal | Animal type label | Hen, Sheep | Any additional species labels used by operations | confirmed examples are non-exhaustive |
| sexe | Sex label | Female, Male | Any other operational labels if present | confirmed examples are non-exhaustive |
| age | Age value of animal | none shown | Numeric or text format depending on implementation | meaning inferred, unit not confirmed |
| etat_sante | Health state label | Sick, Healthy | Any additional health labels used in real data | do not treat observed values as complete list |
| statut | Operational/commercial status label | For Sale, Retained | Any additional status labels used in business workflow | observed values are examples only |
| id_elevage | Reference to livestock unit | none shown | n/a | inferred relational role |

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned > Table: vaccinations
text:
## Table: vaccinations

| Field | Business Meaning | confirmed_examples_from_screenshot | candidate_values_unconfirmed | Confidence Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| id_vaccination | Technical identifier for one vaccination record | none shown | n/a | inferred key role |
| vaccine_name | Vaccine name label | Brucellosis | Any other vaccine names present in database | examples are not exhaustive |
| date_done | Date when action was done | none shown | Any valid date format configured by DB/application | meaning inferred from naming |
| date_next | Next planned date | none shown | Any valid date format configured by DB/application | meaning inferred from naming |
| notes | Free text notes | none shown | Any text notes entered by users | inferred from naming |
| status | Vaccination status label | Scheduled | Any additional workflow labels used in real usage | only Scheduled is currently observed |
| id_animal | Reference to animal | none shown | n/a | inferred relational role |

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned > Table: elevage
text:
## Table: elevage

| Field | Business Meaning | confirmed_examples_from_screenshot | candidate_values_unconfirmed | Confidence Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| id_elevage | Technical identifier for livestock unit | none shown | n/a | inferred key role |
| type_elevage | Livestock type label | Poultry Farm, Sheep Farm, Bovin Farm | Any additional farm-type labels | examples are non-exhaustive |
| etat_elevage | Livestock state/condition label | Cleaning, Under Cleaning | Any additional operational condition labels | avoid assuming severity taxonomy |
| capacite | Capacity value | none shown | Numeric values according to business setup | inferred from naming |
| nombre_animaux | Current number of animals | none shown | Numeric values according to business setup | inferred from naming |
| production | Production label/value | Egg, Milk | Any additional production labels or metrics | observed examples only |
| latitude | Geographic latitude | none shown | Decimal coordinate values | inferred from naming |
| longitude | Geographic longitude | none shown | Decimal coordinate values | inferred from naming |

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned > Similar Concepts Clarified
text: ## Similar Concepts Clarified

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned > Similar Concepts Clarified > etat_sante vs statut (animals)
text:
### etat_sante vs statut (animals)
- etat_sante: health-oriented label.
- statut: operational or commercial placement label.

Confidence: inferred from field naming and observed sample values.

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned > Similar Concepts Clarified > vaccinations.status vs elevage.etat_elevage
text:
### vaccinations.status vs elevage.etat_elevage
- vaccinations.status: workflow label for a vaccination record.
- etat_elevage: condition label for a livestock unit.

Confidence: inferred from entity context.

section: Data Dictionary - Evidence Aligned > Multilingual Guidance
text:
## Multilingual Guidance
- Keep original French field names in retrieval metadata and answers.
- Support query matching with French and English wording (for example elevage/livestock, etat_sante/health state).

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant
text: # FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Confidence Reminder
text:
## Confidence Reminder
Answers below follow the conservative model:
- confirmed where screenshot evidence exists,
- inferred where structure suggests behavior,
- assumed only when clearly labeled.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q1: What data domains are confirmed right now?
text:
## Q1: What data domains are confirmed right now?
Confirmed tables are animals, vaccinations, and elevage.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q2: Are the observed values fixed enums?
text:
## Q2: Are the observed values fixed enums?
No. The observed values are sample values from screenshots and are not exhaustive.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q3: What values were observed for animals.type_animal?
text:
## Q3: What values were observed for animals.type_animal?
Observed samples: Hen, Sheep.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q4: What values were observed for animals.statut?
text:
## Q4: What values were observed for animals.statut?
Observed samples: For Sale, Retained.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q5: What value was observed for vaccinations.status?
text:
## Q5: What value was observed for vaccinations.status?
Observed sample: Scheduled.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q6: Is Scheduled the only status possible?
text:
## Q6: Is Scheduled the only status possible?
Unknown. Only Scheduled is confirmed as observed; other values may exist.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q7: What values were observed for elevage.etat_elevage?
text:
## Q7: What values were observed for elevage.etat_elevage?
Observed samples: Cleaning, Under Cleaning.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q8: Can I treat Cleaning and Under Cleaning as a complete state model?
text:
## Q8: Can I treat Cleaning and Under Cleaning as a complete state model?
No. They are observed examples only.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q9: Are primary keys and foreign keys confirmed?
text:
## Q9: Are primary keys and foreign keys confirmed?
Not fully. Key roles are inferred from naming but should be confirmed via SQL DDL.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q10: How are animals and vaccinations connected?
text:
## Q10: How are animals and vaccinations connected?
Inferred link: vaccinations.id_animal likely references animals.id_animal.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q11: How are animals and elevage connected?
text:
## Q11: How are animals and elevage connected?
Inferred link: animals.id_elevage likely references elevage.id_elevage.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q12: Can the assistant provide strict overdue policy now?
text:
## Q12: Can the assistant provide strict overdue policy now?
Not as a confirmed policy. It can provide a hypothetical interpretation and ask for validation.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q13: Can the assistant answer in French?
text:
## Q13: Can the assistant answer in French?
Yes. The corpus is multilingual-aware and keeps French schema names.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q14: Why is confidence labeling important in this project?
text:
## Q14: Why is confidence labeling important in this project?
Because current evidence is limited, confidence labels prevent hallucinated certainty and support safe production rollout.

section: FAQ - Evidence Aligned Farm Assistant > Q15: What should be confirmed before enforcing business rules?
text:
## Q15: What should be confirmed before enforcing business rules?
- SQL DDL constraints,
- official status/state vocabularies,
- date interpretation policy,
- any threshold-based alert policy.

section: Glossary - Confirmed vs Candidate Vocabulary > B) candidate_vocabulary_unconfirmed

## B) candidate_vocabulary_unconfirmed
The following terms may be useful in future workflows but are not confirmed as official database labels:
- overdue
- upcoming
- delayed
- compliance
- risk score
- occupancy alert

Use these only as explanatory language, not as canonical data values, until validated.

section: Glossary - Confirmed vs Candidate Vocabulary > C) inferred_semantic_pairs

- etat_sante <-> health state
- statut <-> operational/commercial status
- elevage <-> livestock unit

Confidence: inferred from schema naming and project context.

section: Glossary - Confirmed vs Candidate Vocabulary > D) multilingual_guideline
text:
## D) multilingual_guideline
- Preserve original French field names in outputs.
- Allow bilingual interpretation in user-facing explanations.
- Avoid translating field names when referencing exact database columns.

section: Livestock Domain (Elevage) - Evidence Aligned Guide
text: # Livestock Domain (Elevage) - Evidence Aligned Guide

section: Livestock Domain (Elevage) - Evidence Aligned Guide > Scope
text:
## Scope
This guide focuses on the elevage domain with strict separation between evidence and assumptions.

section: Livestock Domain (Elevage) - Evidence Aligned Guide > confirmed_from_screenshot
text:
## confirmed_from_screenshot
- Table exists: elevage.
- Confirmed fields: id_elevage, type_elevage, etat_elevage, capacite, nombre_animaux, production, latitude, longitude.
- Observed sample values (not exhaustive):
  - type_elevage: Poultry Farm, Sheep Farm, Bovin Farm
  - etat_elevage: Cleaning, Under Cleaning
  - production: Egg, Milk

section: Livestock Domain (Elevage) - Evidence Aligned Guide > inferred_from_schema
text:
## inferred_from_schema
- elevage likely represents a livestock unit or farm unit context.
- animals.id_elevage likely references elevage.id_elevage.
- capacite and nombre_animaux likely support occupancy monitoring.
- latitude and longitude likely support mapping and geolocation-based views.

section: Livestock Domain (Elevage) - Evidence Aligned Guide > assumed_business_rule
text:
## assumed_business_rule
No official severity framework for etat_elevage is confirmed.

Important correction:
- Do not treat labels such as stable/warning/critical as canonical unless confirmed by owner or DDL/business docs.
- Current observed labels (Cleaning, Under Cleaning) must be treated as sample values only.

Hypothetical examples (not policy):
- Teams may use etat_elevage for maintenance or operational activity states.
- Teams may compute occupancy indicators from capacite and nombre_animaux.

section: Livestock Domain (Elevage) - Evidence Aligned Guide > Monitoring Questions Supported By Current Evidence
text:
## Monitoring Questions Supported By Current Evidence
- What are the confirmed fields of elevage?
- Which etat_elevage values were observed in screenshots?
- Is the observed value list exhaustive? (answer: no)
- How does elevage connect to animals?

section: Livestock Domain (Elevage) - Evidence Aligned Guide > Multilingual Notes
text:
## Multilingual Notes
- Preserve French field names in prompts and answers.
- Accept both elevage and livestock wording in user queries.

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage)
text: # Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage)

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > Scope
text:
## Scope
This reference documents only what is currently supported by screenshot evidence and cautious schema inference.

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > Confidence Taxonomy
text:
## Confidence Taxonomy
- confirmed: visible in phpMyAdmin screenshots.
- inferred: logical inference from table/field structure and naming.
- assumed: hypothetical business policy that still needs owner validation.

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > confirmed_from_screenshot
text: ## confirmed_from_screenshot

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > confirmed_from_screenshot > Table: animals
text:
### Table: animals
Confirmed fields:
- id_animal
- type_animal
- sexe
- age
- etat_sante
- statut
- id_elevage

Observed sample values (not exhaustive):
- type_animal: Hen, Sheep
- sexe: Female, Male
- etat_sante: Sick, Healthy
- statut: For Sale, Retained

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > confirmed_from_screenshot > Table: vaccinations
text:
### Table: vaccinations
Confirmed fields:
- id_vaccination
- vaccine_name
- date_done
- date_next
- notes
- status
- id_animal

Observed sample values (not exhaustive):
- vaccine_name: Brucellosis
- status: Scheduled

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > confirmed_from_screenshot > Table: elevage
text:
### Table: elevage
Confirmed fields:
- id_elevage
- type_elevage
- etat_elevage
- capacite
- nombre_animaux
- production
- latitude
- longitude

Observed sample values (not exhaustive):
- type_elevage: Poultry Farm, Sheep Farm, Bovin Farm
- etat_elevage: Cleaning, Under Cleaning
- production: Egg, Milk

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > inferred_from_schema
text: ## inferred_from_schema

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > inferred_from_schema > Likely key roles (not yet confirmed by SQL DDL)
text:
### Likely key roles (not yet confirmed by SQL DDL)
- animals.id_animal likely acts as primary key.
- vaccinations.id_vaccination likely acts as primary key.
- elevage.id_elevage likely acts as primary key.

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > inferred_from_schema > Likely relationships (not yet confirmed by SQL constraints)
text:
### Likely relationships (not yet confirmed by SQL constraints)
- animals.id_elevage likely references elevage.id_elevage.
- vaccinations.id_animal likely references animals.id_animal.

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > inferred_from_schema > Likely cardinality
text:
### Likely cardinality
- One elevage to many animals.
- One animal to many vaccinations.

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > assumed_business_rule
text:
## assumed_business_rule
No canonical policy rules are asserted in this file.

Only a technical assumption for future checks:
- If SQL DDL confirms FK constraints, referential integrity should be enforced at database level.

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > Entity Data Flow (Conservative)
text:
## Entity Data Flow (Conservative)
1. An elevage record defines livestock context (type, state label, capacity, production label, geolocation).
2. Animal records are associated to elevage through id_elevage.
3. Vaccination records are associated to animals through id_animal.

This flow is suitable for future cross-entity retrieval and analytics, but exact enforcement rules remain to be validated.

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > Multilingual Note
text:
## Multilingual Note
The schema uses French field names while observed values may be English. RAG processing must preserve original field names and support bilingual querying (French and English wording).

section: Schema Reference - Evidence Aligned (Animals, Vaccination, Elevage) > Validation Gaps To Close Next
text:
## Validation Gaps To Close Next
- Export and review full SQL DDL for all three tables.
- Confirm actual PK/FK constraints and indexes.
- Confirm data types and nullability for date and status fields.
- Confirm whether displayed values are free text or controlled labels.

section: Vaccination Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide
text: # Vaccination Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide

section: Vaccination Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > Scope
text:
## Scope
This guide documents vaccination data conservatively, without promoting unverified workflows as canon.

section: Vaccination Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > confirmed_from_screenshot
text:
## confirmed_from_screenshot
- Table exists: vaccinations.
- Confirmed fields: id_vaccination, vaccine_name, date_done, date_next, notes, status, id_animal.
- Observed sample values (not exhaustive):
  - vaccine_name: Brucellosis
  - status: Scheduled

section: Vaccination Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > inferred_from_schema
text:
## inferred_from_schema
- vaccinations.id_animal likely links vaccination records to animals.id_animal.
- date_done and date_next likely support historical and planning views.
- notes likely stores contextual observations entered by staff.

section: Vaccination Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > assumed_business_rule
text:
## assumed_business_rule
No official status lifecycle is confirmed from screenshots beyond observed sample value Scheduled.

Do not treat undocumented labels as canonical.

Hypothetical workflow examples (require validation):
- date_next may be used to prepare upcoming tasks.
- date_done may indicate completed administration date.
- status labels may vary across teams and periods.

section: Vaccination Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > Practical Questions Supported By Current Evidence
text:
## Practical Questions Supported By Current Evidence
- What fields are available in vaccination records?
- Which vaccination status value is currently observed? (Scheduled)
- Is Scheduled the only possible status? (unknown, not exhaustive)
- How are vaccination records connected to animals?

section: Vaccination Domain - Evidence Aligned Guide > Multilingual Notes
text:
## Multilingual Notes
- Keep French schema names in data references.
- Support bilingual query wording, for example vaccination status and statut de vaccination.
